title: Exercise test result review

investigations.text: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.
investigations.rationale: The NICE stable chest-pain section supports documenting the reason and context for reviewed diagnostic testing, symptoms and risk context, and clinician interpretation of an existing exercise-related result without automatically ordering a test or diagnosing ischemia.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

investigations.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
investigations.rationale: The information section supports documenting clinician explanation of a reviewed result, uncertainty, questions, shared decisions, safety-netting, and arranged follow-up without generating advice.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

source_limitations: Indication, protocol, date, exercise duration and workload, heart rate and blood pressure response, symptoms, ECG changes, arrhythmia, reason for stopping, imaging if combined, technical quality, formal interpretation, diagnosis, risk, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
